frameworks.name: operant conditioning
frameworks.one_line: shaping center-port fixation by progressive reinforcement of hold duration

frameworks.name: reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: curriculum design rationalized by RL-style reward-driven skill acquisition

core_question: Can a standardized training curriculum efficiently shape robust center-port fixation behavior in rodents while minimizing motor violation errors?
key_assumptions: longer initial fixation requirement aids learning
delaying violation penalties protects early learning
manipulations: initial required fixation duration (long vs short)
session-boundary increments of required fixation duration
key_findings: low violation rates were achieved at the end of training
the curriculum generalized across rodent species and across task difficulties
interpretations: a standardized curriculum can replace ad-hoc shaping for fixation tasks
curriculum design is well supported by operant conditioning and reinforcement learning principles
